column1147: 10/Sep/24 12:07;kgugerty;Currently investigating connection to PingFed;;;
column1148: 10/Sep/24 13:31;crivas;Looks like the server memory distribution was set to round robin and not to evenly distribute the load. Tech Ops working with AWS to set this up correctly.;;;
column1149: 10/Sep/24 13:32;crivas;Pending as clients are reporting login issues have cleared up. User Logins are now successful. ;;;
column1150:
10/Sep/24 17:04;kgugerty;High level summary of the issue:

The main issue was the performance on one of the Domain Controllers maxed out this morning.  PingFed was setup to send traffic to a primary domain controller, then failover to the next domain controller if there were issues.  The steady increase of AppStream clients over the period of the migration maxed out the capacity of the domain controller this morning.  It failed over to another controller as designed, but moved all traffic with it thus maxing it out as well. 

The immediate resolution was to setup PingFed to assign the traffic to the domain controllers on round-robin basis to break up the load.  Near Term resolution, overnight, the Domain Controller resources will be increased to handle the additional capacity.  Long Term Resolution is to implement load balancing.

Additionally, logs are being reviewed to ensure there wasn't some sort of denial of service attack from a bad actor.;;;

column1156:
28/Jun/24 10:57;jmurphy;From [~mfelde]
*Issue Synopsis*
Performance issue with stored procedure:  sp_MOM_WSTool_GetEstimateHeaderAndMarketsAndOrdersForEstimate
Outdated query plan causing fullscan of verify large tables.  i.e.) DAYPART, SPOT
*Steps taken to remediate the issue:*
Updated Table(s) Statistics
* PROCESS_OFFER
* PROCESS_STATION_VERSION
* PROCESS_STATION
* PROCESS_SURVEY
* PROCESS_DAYPART
* PROCESS_SPOT
* PROCESS_ESTIMATE
* PROCESS_OFFER_DAYPART
* PROCESS_OFFER_SPOT
* DAYPART
* SPOT
Force/Pinned an old valid query execution plan.
*Future updates to limit issue from occurring:*
* Look into disabling AUTO_UPDATE_STATISTICS for the given tables.
* Look into using query execution hints to limit fullscans.  i.e.) FORCESEEK query hint(s) for DAYPART and SPOT tables to limit fullscans.;;;

column29:
*{color:#ff8b00}RCA Request{color}*

 

Issue:

User Amar Pehlwan from Evergreen Trading Company is in LedgerSync and attempted to run Monthly Accruals for December 2023 from Horizon prod. This is a short list of some of the expected estimate numbers 75500, 76882, 77715, 77716, 77718, 77723, 77724, 23535. This is across various media types Radio, National Cable.

This appears to be because the script requested on OPS-177459 was run on the production database and not just UAT which was Erin's intention. This is confirmed by applying a filter to the stored procedure folder searching for a name containing 'accruals'. You can see that FWS-41959 is noted in the text when you right-click on it and do a 'Script stored prod as' -> Create to -> New Query Window (this was the method Erin showed me to confirm if the script had been run in prod on SQLW6\I1). This needs to be rectified so that Evergreen data is populated (which it currently is not).

PER ERIN, "[June] said she can write a script to repopulate Evergreen data so they can pull it. Can you add a request in your ticket to techops to get a copy of HMI's database restored to a network server so she can test her script before we deploy?" Can we please get a copy of their production SBMSDATA database restored somewhere product can test this script against?

Primary Case ID
 * 
 ** 00835235- case that prompted Jira
 * Jiras: OPS-181025
 * Total Impacted Clients: 1 agency
 * Total Cases: 1

 * Priority Clients Names & Case Count
 ** Evergreen Trading Company- 1 case

 *  
 * First report:
 ** 1/3/2024 12:12 PM- 835235- first Agency side case submitted for issue. (Agency support hours 8am CST-7pm CST)
 * Resolved:

 * 
 ** The Product/Dev team created a Script fix that TechOps ran tonight. Service has updated the client.
 * Resolution:
 ** TechOps is working on getting a database backup so it can be restored and a new script can be written by the Product team.
 ** New script has been written and tested on a Production backup. Waiting for TechOps to deploy. 
 * Internal Stakeholders:
 ** Chen/Erin/JK/Tricia/Heather/Krystian

Root Cause: TBD

column1147:
30/Dec/24 13:28;MMurray;The users that were deactivated all had Effective End Date's in the Setup Users UI page set to 12/29/2024. There is a Scheduled Task job "Update User Active Flag" that runs daily at 5am and set all of the users to Active = False. Brad Biggs from Effectv had attempted to update all the effected users Effective End Date to 1/3/2025 but doing so from Mass Edit does not also update the Active flag to True and there is no Active checkbox available in the Mass Edit dialog box. Doing the same action to a single user in the Setup UI does update the Active flag to True and I think this was done for some users that had called previous to the P2 issue being created.

The original decision was going to be to take the list of User Id's that Brad Biggs provided and update the setup User/AccountExecutive tables to set the Active flag to True for those users via script. However thanks to [~BFitzgerald] for saving the time and seeing the same Scheduled Task job code not only deactivates users with Effective End Dates in the past but also re-activates them if their Effective End Date is in the future and they're set to Active = False.

So the job was kicked off (screenshot below) and after it ran I verified in the DB that the effected users had their Active flags set to 1 (True). The UI still wasn't showing the same so an IIS Reset was performed by Eric Nielsen of Effectv on all 10 Prod web servers and then once logged in with a fresh session the UI reflected the same changes. Brad Biggs confirmed he saw the same and will discuss with Mehran whether they move the Effective End Dates to some date far in the future or blank it out entirely but that can be done through the Setup UI.

!image-2024-12-30-12-26-48-824.png|width=1005,height=243!;;;
column1148:
30/Dec/24 17:18;MMurray;It can be seen on the OneSuite Setup Users page [https://onetimprod.cable.comcast.com/Setup/Home/Index/Users] that the previously deactivated users now show as Active and it was also confirmed by Brad Biggs at Effectv so moving the case to Done status.

!image-2024-12-30-16-18-11-692.png|width=942,height=615!;;;

column10:
Project for all support activities escalated to engineering team.

column29:
Regarding FWS-51194 the weekly sync job failed for the 2nd week in a row. 

Had a call with Jessie, Jignesh, Kelly, and myself troubleshooting on the server with Kelly providing log file errors that showed the jobs still failing on writing the FTP downloads to the E:\Trib and E:\Trib2 folder. There was a third folder E:\Trib3 that likely had the same error but we didn't check that specifically. The error is as follows:

OnError,,,Download,\{6CFB8E14-E715-41F5-A57C-3A9B5E0D75D6},,10/6/2024 1:12:40 AM,10/6/2024 1:12:40 AM,-1073573551,0x,In Executing "C:\Program Files\PuTTY\psftp.exe" "cnt566yw@ftp.tmstv.com -pw HRB696dg -hostkey SHA256:GpFtinB/fcMY1koUh95b0DqI3zX/r9ErlEQXqWbUxUE -b C:\PackageConfigs\ImportTribune.ftp" at "E:\Trib", The process exit code was "1" while the expected was "0".

OnError,,,Load Tribune Flat Files v2 2,\{2B4F43DF-4AA2-4BD2-B63A-36CDF6AF38EF},,10/6/2024 1:12:40 AM,10/6/2024 1:12:40 AM,-1073573551,0x,In Executing "C:\Program Files\PuTTY\psftp.exe" "cnt566yw@ftp.tmstv.com -pw HRB696dg -hostkey SHA256:GpFtinB/fcMY1koUh95b0DqI3zX/r9ErlEQXqWbUxUE -b C:\PackageConfigs\ImportTribune.ftp" at "E:\Trib", The process exit code was "1" while the expected was "0".

Since the errors referenced C:\PackageConfigs\ImportTribune.ftp we had Kelly open that file and confirmed the contents

lcd E:\Trib
cd bigb
get advcprogrec.txt.gz
get advcskedrec.txt.gz
get advcstatrec.txt.gz
quit

With the error referencing the "{*}at E:\Trib{*}" ** it appears there's an issue with permissions to those Trib, Trib2, Trib3 folders and the ability for whatever user runs the SSIS job to write the downloaded files there.

11/Oct/24 14:47;MMurray;[~cphuah] [~kpham] - We are planning to do a manual run of the job that is failing but we would first like to request the Permissions on the E:\Trib, E:\Trib2, and E:\Trib3 folders to *temporarily* add "Everyone" to have Full Control access. This would only be for the duration of the manual run which is about 90 minutes. If it succeeds then we will confirm that the issue with the job is Permissions based and figure out which user (aka not Everyone) needs to be added to those folders and add that user instead for future runs.

Please have this done ASAP as this issue is blocking SOS from updating program information from Trib data.;;;

column1149:

For reference we checked the SSIS job executions report for the failed job in order to see what user was in the "Caller" field and it is SSISAdmin which was already added to have write permissions on the Trib, Trib2, Trib3 folders. Attaching the screenshots showing that to the ticket.;;;
column1151:
11/Oct/24 14:48;MMurray;Job was manually kicked off by Kelly this morning while we were on a call. Was just informed from Erica on the SOS side that the job failed.

[~kpham] will need to get on a call again today request the log and see if it's the same error message.;;;
column1152:
11/Oct/24 14:48;MMurray;[~cphuah] [~kpham] 

Thanks to Linda Zhu who found the document that is now attached to this ticket showing the "How To Install" the weekly process with setting the permissions on the FTP folders. We will likely need to step through this document on the Prod SOS DB server to get it set.;;;
column1153:
11/Oct/24 14:48;MMurray;Added the _RE SOS Prod DB Troubleshooting for Weekly Process Job Failure.msg_ email message thread to the ticket to show the troubleshooting steps done yesterday. Kelly was unable to perform two updates due to elevated permissions and we need those to be run/updated today.

[~cphuah] please see the two changes below:

1) In SSMS for the SOS Prod DB (SQL-SOS-19.gotostrata.com) go to Security --> Credentials --> SSISCred and right-click to go to Properties. There the SSIS Admin is set. It was mentioned that something happened with that users credentials so we would need them reset there. During a previous call Chen reset that user's credentials somewhere else on the server but I don't think this spot was hit.

2) Also on the SOS Prod DB navigate to SQL Server Agent --> Proxies --> SSIS Package Execution --> SSISProxy and right-click to go to Properties. Under the Principals section click Add... and add the NT Service\SQLSERVERAGENT and SQL-SOS-19\SSISAdmin users. Click OK for the Add Principal window and for the Properties window. After doing so go back into the Properties and ensure the users were added. Kelly performed this action but upon re-checking the add did not stick.

Third change may be possible for Kelly to perform so we can try with her permissions first. When running the job ProcessMonitor was run and captured any "ACCESS DENIED" messages that occurred on the server. Many RegOpenKey errors were found and Jessie saw a particular spot that should be added in the Registry. 

3) Open RegEdit and navigate to Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services and open Permissions to grant "ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES" Full Control instead of just Read access. Additionally navigate to Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\SystemCertificates and grant the same ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES Full Control.

Will attach the ProcessMonitor log file directly to the ticket along with screenshots from the email.;;;
column1154:
11/Oct/24 17:18;jmurphy;*Update: *The troubleshooting team was able to determine that a hostkey update by external vendor/partner Nielsen/Gracenote on 9/28/2024 is the root cause of the failure.   The next step is for the dev team to update the installation package with the updated hostkey, validate this change and deploy this updated package in the production environment.

The dev team is currently working to update the installation package and validate the change.  This process is active and underway.;;;
column1155: 11/Oct/24 19:27;jmurphy;The dev team is working to update the installation package and validation this change on Monday (10/14) will the target goal to resolve this issue that same day.;;;
column1156: 14/Oct/24 18:12;jmurphy;The team was able to update the configuration (to update the new Nielsen hostkey value) and successfully run the job.  Validate is underway.;;;

column10:

column29:
Users log in to buy.gotostrata.com and get a message asking the user to Continue with Browser or Open AppStream 2.0 client, after entering creds and clicking Login. If users select Continue with Browser, they eventually get stuck at a message that says they can close the browser. There is no way for affected users to access AppStream currently.

Details gathered so far:
-Confirmed users accessing buy.gotostrata.com
-Heather (heather.m.schmeckpeper@gmail.com) able to reproduce the issue on her Mac with Chrome (v126.0.6478.127), Safari (v17.5), and Firefox (128.0 64 bit)
-Reported by 13+ clients so far: Crossroads, TCA, LLC (5686), Crossroads Media (3870), Shine United LLC (4645), Seneca Gaming (5812), TriSpark (4166), Swanson Russell (3235), Brandon Agency Inc (4050), FP1 Strategies LLC (5655), K&H, LLC (1291), AxMedia (4905), MJS Advertising (2324), Flynn Agency, LLC (formerly Martino Flynn) (3472), Access Media Services (4445)
-Gathered affected user's credentials and tried to log in as the user here and could not reproduce the issue.
-Logoff, recreate profile, Unlock, PW reset, Incognito/Private mode, clearing history/cache had no effect. Citrix is the only workaround.

As a result of this issue, Citrix has been turned back on for all wave 1 and wave 2 clients that had it turned off.

12/Mar/24 20:27;tstrege;[~kgugerty] 
The server reboot was successful.  Additional memory allocations took effect right away.  Unfortunately, the firewall exceptions were reset.  I added port 9999 back in but was unaware of the requirement for port 19999.  This was added back in just after the outage reported at 4:20pm CST.  I set the exceptions so they should persist through another reboot in the future.

There was an additional issue with the VPN not resolving the [https://lookerbi.gotostrata.com:9999|https://lookerbi.gotostrata.com/] address.  This is an internal DNS resolution issue and does not affect the client access.

column1151: 13/Mar/24 12:40;durso;Pending this ticket based on Tricia's recent comment, can be reopened if need be;;;
column1152: 13/Mar/24 14:48;tstrege;[~kgugerty] Can you please add your Root Cause Analysis to this ticket?;;;
column1153:
13/Mar/24 15:43;kgugerty;RCA -

The shortened timeline on the AWS transition led inadequate research on AWS Instance Types and their compatibility.

The looker server was then moved to a T2 type instance that did not have ENA support.  The T2 Type instance also runs on CPU Credits that allows burst-able performance.  Credits accumulate over time and are used when the server hits certain thresholds.  The looker server was under provisioned and used up it's CPU Credits more quickly at Peak usage than it could replenish during non-peak usage.  This led to bottle neck performance at the baseline CPU allotment.

 OPS-185487 brought this issue to light and we needed to change the instance type.  The lack of ENA drivers on the current looker server limited the ability to migrate the server to a new instance type.  The decision was made to stand up a new server and migrate the application.

The application was brought over and tested over the weekend of 3/9-3/10.  On Monday 3/11 the application encountered an error with memory allocation.  Looker was updated to allow more memory usage and the server rebooted.  The reboot caused firewall settings to be cleared and needed to be added back in.  Port 9999 was added in allowing Web UI access, but 19999 (which allows api access) was missed.  The outage was reported in this ticket at 4:20pm CST and the 19999 port was discovered and added back in. 

 

Resolutions:

AWS Instance Types research ([https://aws.amazon.com/ec2/instance-types/)]

Nagios Monitoring added for port 19999 (previously existed for port 9999);;;
